summary: McKenzie reports a low-productivity day during his 30-day development sprint, having only sketched some interface screens after a long workday and typhoon-delayed commute. He highlights other participants in the 30-day challenge, particularly a poker graphing utility, noting his curiosity about marketing in a spam-saturated niche. The post is a brief daily check-in reflecting the reality that not every day in a sprint produces meaningful output.
key_points: Long work hours and external factors like weather can derail side-project productivity on any given day
Marketing a legitimate product in a niche dominated by scammers is an interesting and difficult challenge
Community accountability through shared development sprints helps maintain momentum even on low-output days
money_quotes: After getting out of work at 8 PM (early) and back home at 12 PM (typhoon) I find myself with not an amazing amount of productive energy for today.
I'd be interested in seeing how he plans to promote something cleanly in an online field saturated with scum and villany.
key_insight: Even during focused development sprints, the reality of a day job and life circumstances means some days produce little more than throwaway sketches, and that's acceptable.
